prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, honey, think of it like the brain of your computer. It’s that little chip inside that does all the thinking and figuring out, just like how I plan our grocery list or solve a crossword puzzle. When you tap a key or click on a picture, it’s the CPU that reads your wish and tells all the other parts what to do. It works very, very fast—much faster than I can knit a scarf! Without it, your computer would just be a quiet box with a pretty screen.
